name: Kabuli Pulao
description: Afghanistan's national dish – fragrant basmati rice cooked with lamb, topped with raisins, carrots and pistachios.

name: Ashak
description: Delicate dumplings filled with chives and leeks, served with spiced minced meat and a garlicky yoghurt sauce.

name: Mantu
description: Afghan steamed dumplings filled with spiced onion and minced lamb, served with split peas and a dill-yoghurt sauce.

name: Bolani
description: Crispy pan-fried flatbread stuffed with potatoes, leeks, or spinach – a popular street food and appetiser.

name: Shorwa
description: Hearty lamb and vegetable soup, a warming staple in the high-altitude provinces.

name: Korma
description: Rich slow-cooked lamb or beef stew with onions, tomatoes and warming spices – Herat style is particularly celebrated.

name: Aush
description: Northern Afghan noodle soup with minced lamb, kidney beans, and a tangy yoghurt and tomato sauce.

name: Sheer Yakh
description: Traditional Afghan ice cream made with rose water, pistachios and saffron – a summer treat from Kabul's old bazaars.

name: Firni
description: Creamy rosewater and cardamom milk pudding set in clay cups – Afghanistan's most beloved dessert.

name: Qabuli Palaw
description: Northern variation of pulao with a more generous use of dried fruits and nuts, often served at celebrations.
